title: MidsReborn Character Build Data
description: Schema for the modern JSON build format produced by MidsReborn's File -> Save As -> JSON. Hand-authored from MidsReborn/Core/BuildFile/CharacterBuildData.cs and the DataModels classes (read as documentation). When MidsReborn updates the C# classes, this file may need a touch-up.

exemplar_10_playability.description: Universal house rule — every build emitted by Claude must remain PLAYABLE when exemplared to level 10. Playable means: character can attack, has a defensive layer running where the AT has one, has a travel power, isn't dead weight. NOT optimized, NOT soft-capped, NOT preserving set bonuses. Level-50 endgame quality is the primary objective; this rule must never trade level-50 quality for exemplar performance.
priority_order: 1. Level-50 endgame quality is the primary build target. Pick powers and enhancements that maximize level-50 performance.
2. Exemplar-10 must be playable (functional, not optimal).
3. Never trade level-50 quality for exemplar bonuses or set-bonus persistence.
checks: PowerEntries with Level <= 14 cover the build's functional core: at least 2 attacks, at least 1 defensive layer (where the AT has one), travel power picked by Level <= 12, Build Up / Aim equivalent if the AT has one, sustain mechanic if the AT depends on one. Most level-50 builds satisfy this naturally because normal leveling picks these in order.
Slot allocation: SlotEntries with Level <= 15 populate exemplar-10-available powers, not just late-game heavy hitters.

Enhancement choices: pick the IOs that maximize level 50. Prefer attuned IOs only when they're as good as the crafted alternative for level 50 (the exemplar-friendly behavior is then a free side benefit). Do NOT pick PvP sets for exemplar set-bonus persistence; PvP set bonuses retain on exemplar but that's informational, not a slotting recommendation. Use purples and crafted level-50 IOs freely when they're best at 50; loss of set bonuses below effective 47 is acceptable.
Trade-off surfacing: when exemplar playability and level-50 optimization conflict, level 50 wins. Explain the trade-off in the build summary and proceed.

BuiltWith.description: Provenance: which MidsReborn version + database produced this build.

Level.description: Character level as a string (1-50). Typed as string in MidsReborn for legacy reasons.

Class.description: Archetype ClassName (e.g. 'Class_Tanker', 'Class_Blaster'). Resolves through Archetypes.json. Required.

Origin.description: Origin: Magic, Mutation, Natural, Science, Technology. Required.

Comment.description: Build notes / changelog the player wrote in the app.

PowerSets.description: Powerset full names in fixed slot order: [Primary, Secondary, Pool1, Pool2, Pool3, Pool4, Ancillary]. Empty string for unfilled slots. Required.

LastPower.description: Index of the last main power in PowerEntries. Required.

PowerEntries.description: Every power slot in the build, including empty slots and inherent powers. Required.

PowerData.description: One power pick (or empty slot) in the build.

PowerName.description: Power FullName (e.g. 'Tanker.Invulnerability.Unstoppable'). Empty string for unfilled slot.

Level.description: Character level at which this power was picked (1-50). -1 means unfilled / inherent at default level.

StatInclude.description: Whether this power's stats are included in the in-app totals view.

ProcInclude.description: Whether procs slotted in this power are included in the in-app totals.

VariableValue.description: For powers with a variable scale (e.g. Defiant Barrage, Power Boost stack count).

InherentSlotsUsed.description: Slots the inherent system has used (Health, Stamina, etc. with their pre-allocated slot levels).

SubPowerEntries.description: Granted sub-powers (auras, secondary form-shifts, etc.). Most powers have none.

SlotEntries.description: Enhancement slots in this power. Length 0..6 (1 inherent + up to 5 added).

SlotData.description: One enhancement slot in a power.

Level.description: Character level at which this slot was added. For the inherent slot, this is the power's pick level.

IsInherent.description: True for the slot that comes free with the power; false for added slots.

Enhancement.description: The enhancement currently slotted. Null = empty slot.

FlippedEnhancement.description: Alternate-form enhancement (e.g. Attuned vs Crafted; Mids stores both for the in-app 'flip' feature).

Uid.description: Enhancement UID, e.g. 'Crushing_Impact_Acc/Dmg' or 'Attuned_Luck_of_the_Gambler_E'. Resolves through Enhancement.json.

Grade.description: Enhancement grade. 'None' for set IOs / IOs (grade-less). TO/DO/SO for older types.

IoLevel.description: Crafted IO level (10-50). Ignored for TO/DO/SO/HO and Attuned IOs.

RelativeLevel.description: Effective level relative to character. Boosters push from Even toward PlusFive (max +5).

Obtained.description: Whether the player has actually acquired this enhancement (vs. planning).
